description: lib.rs:56,83,167 + module doc lines 43-48 — Every stored value is Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>, and the module doc explicitly states what this does NOT protect against (memory dumps, no encryption, no access control). This is honest threat modeling: the code does what it can, and the docs tell callers where the boundary is. The security note is not boilerplate — it names specific non-guarantees.
title: Good: Zeroizing throughout with honest threat model
